English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Barlow Ware papers
Ware, Barlow, 1925-2011.
4-3-2587
1.2 cubic feet. 1.2 cubic feet.
1921-2012. Correspondence, photographs, printed material, financial records, memorabilia. Files of Barlow Ware relating to his life and work at Cornell University. Contains files from his work in the Office of Special Projects and Alumni Affairs. These files mostly pertain to fundraising for fraternities; several files contain copies of documents relating to the transfer of the houses to university ownership. Files relating to his personal life including many items regarding his family genealogy from the Ware, Barlow, Effinger families and others, correspondence with family members including many letters written to his mother, Katherine Taylor Effinger, as a child and photographs. Photo albums covering his entire life, including his time at Cornell as a student. Files relating to his support of Cornell include his awards and recognitions including his Hall of Fame and Foremost Benefactor inductions. Also a typescript European travel diary from a lengthy trip in 1956-1957. Also material relating to his support of the glass Blaschka Marine Invertebrate Collection. Other items relate to his work with Rotary International and the Boy Scouts of America. Collection material in English 2094180
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
June 2018
EAD encoding:
Jude Corina, June 2018
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, July 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
William Barlow Ware Jr. graduated from Cornell University in 1947. Ware served as manager to the wrestling team and as Touchdown the Bear. He would later join Cornell to work in Alumni Affairs and Development as director of Special Projects. He also served as the voice of the Big Red as the announcer at hockey and football games. His dedication to Cornell and the Ithaca community through his service and his donations would earn him numerous recognitions including induction into the Cornell Athletic Hall of Fame, recognition as a Foremost Benefactor, the Frank H. T. Rhodes Exemplary Alumni Service Award, Rotary Foundation Meritorious Service Award, Boy Scouts of America Silver Beaver Award, and many others.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Files of Barlow Ware relating to his life and work at Cornell University. Contains files from his work in the Office of Special Projects and Alumni Affairs. These files mostly pertain to fundraising for fraternities; several files contain copies of documents relating to the transfer of the houses to university ownership. Files relating to his personal life including many items regarding his family genealogy from the Ware, Barlow, Effinger families and others, correspondence with family members including many letters written to his mother, Katherine Taylor Effinger, as a child and photographs. Photo albums covering his entire life, including his time at Cornell as a student. Files relating to his support of Cornell include his awards and recognitions including his Hall of Fame and Foremost Benefactor inductions. Also a typescript European travel diary from a lengthy trip in 1956-1957. Also material relating to his support of the glass Blaschka Marine Invertebrate Collection. Other items relate to his work with Rotary International and the Boy Scouts of America.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Office of Special Projects files
Series II. Photograph Albums
Series III. Personal Files Including Genealogy
Series IV. Awards, Recognitions, Ceremonies
SEPARATED MATERIAL
New York Times article from June 15, 1947 titled "The College Graduate-1947 Model
Signed photograph of President Livingston Farrand to Foster Coffin
Cornell blanket
Craigielea Memorial Education Fund of Chi Phi seal.
French engravings, Les deux Grands-Ducs, Un nouveau converti, and Paris enrhume by Honoré Daumier.
Cite As:
Barlow Ware papers, #4-3-2587. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Scrapbooks. Greek letter societies -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Effinger family. Cornell University -- : Students. Boy Scouts of America Quill and Dagger Society (Cornell University) Rotary International Wildegg (Castle) Cornell University. Office of Special Projects Cornell University. Class of 1947
2094180 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. Office of Special Projects recordsAlpha Delta Phi1958-198511Alpha Epsilon Phi198312Chi Phi1971-198013Alpha Phi Alpha198414General fraternity information1975-198215Chi Phi1967-198416Chi Psi1968-196917-8Delta Chi198419Delta Kappa Epsilon1960-1972110Delta Phi1973-1980111Delta Sigma Phi1980-1983112Delta Tau Delta1963-1980113Group Housing funds summary1961-1966114Delta Upsilon1960-1984115Kappa Alpha1958-1980116Kappa Alpha Fund1945-1946117Kappa Alpha Theta1968-1981118Kappa Delta1972-1975119Kappa Sigma Group Housing Fund1973-1976120Phi Gamma Delta1976-1984121Phi Kappa Psi1980-1985122Pi Kappa Phi1983-1985123Psi Upsilon1963-1985124Sigma Alpha Epsilon1980-1985125-27Sigma Alpha Mu1961-1977128Sigma Nu1984129Sigma Phi Epsilon1975130Theta Delta Chi1978-1985131Series II. Photograph AlbumsChildhood photos of Ware19302
Scope and Contents
Personal photos of friends and events.Photo album of Katherine Taylor Effinger1935-19402
Scope and Contents
Family album of labeled photographs of Ware as a child and other family members, friends, and travels.Photo album of Katherine Taylor Effinger1935-19502
Scope and Contents
Family album of photographs of Ware as a child and other family members through his time as a Cornell student. Includes images of travels to Sierra Madre, Washington D.C. and others. Many labeled photographs of friends and family members.Woodberry Forest School and Cornell University album1939-198921
Scope and Contents
Includes photos of student activities, Cornell Dramatic Club photos (Alex Drummond cast Ware in shows 1943-1945, campus buildings, 419 Wyckoff, Janie Caldwell, 1947 Winter ice carving contest, Cornell wrestling, 1947 graduation, Ware's mother and father, travels and family photos, Ware plot at Dale Cementery and Effinger Mausoleum in Thornrose Cemetery, Fiddle-On-Picton cabin in Clayton New York, Cornell Glee Club in Beijing China.,Rotary and personal photo album1988-199322
Scope and Contents
Images of Rotary events, people, trips and personal photos. Includes photos of the Dali Lama.Rotary and personal photo album1992-199523
Scope and Contents
Images of Rotary events, people, trips and personal photos. Cornell Class of 1947 reunionRotary and personal photo album1996-199824
Scope and Contents
Images of Rotary events, people, trips and personal photos.Photo album of Ware's home, interior and exterior, on 524 Wyckoff Road.19952Rotary and personal photo album1998-20032
Scope and Contents
Images of Rotary events, and friends. Photo of three Cornell sports announcers, Elmer "Flip" Phillips '32, Barlow Ware '47, and Arthur Mintz '71.Photo signed by President George Walker Bush. Photos of Cornell Class of 1947 reunion 2002, Ware's Athletic Hall of Fame induction.Alumni affairs photo album20032
Scope and Contents
Possibly in honor of Ware's retirement, but also includes photos from his induction to the Cornell Athletic Hall of Fame and a letter from Ned Harkness.Photo album2003-20062
Scope and Contents
Personal photos of friends and events.Takeuchi family visit photo albumDecember 20022
Scope and Contents
Personal photos of friends and events.Dedication of W. Barlow Ware Bench photo albumSeptember 27, 20082
Scope and Contents
Dedication of stone bench outside of Teagle Hall.Foremost Benefactor albumMay 8, 20063
Scope and Contents
Album containing programs and photographs relating to Ware's recognition event as a foremost benefactor of Cornell University. Includes photographs of Ware, former Presidents Frank H. T. Rhodes and Dale Corson, Andy Noel and many other friends and athletic and alumni affairs staff. Photos show the presentation of the Ezra Cornell statute and Ware's inscription on the Builder's Wall. Also a number of letters with memories and thanks to Ware. Some copies of photos from early in Ware's Cornell career as well.Series III. Personal Files Including GenealogyBiographical informationUndated41
Scope and Contents
Photos, employment history, reflections on his 50th ReunionPersonal files1946-200342
Scope and Contents
Includes letters relating to items in his home, his Rolodex cards, correspondence, some relating to the Cornell Wrestling team, invitation letter for his induction into the Quill and Dagger Society, photo of him as Touchdown the bear and a letter from Herb Roth Sr. about Ware being the bear.Barlow family genealogyUndated43
Scope and Contents
Includes information and family trees on Brewster, Bradford, Laflin, Richmond and other surnames, descendent chart of William Henry Barlow, notes compiled by Edward Richmond Ware, History of the Episcopal Church in Sing Sing by William Henry Barlow, info on Howard Barlow, correspondence, publications.Ware family genealogy1892-198544
Scope and Contents
Information and family trees related to the Ware family. Many items relating to or from Edward Richmond Ware including baptism and birth records, military service records, pamphlet by him on the Barlow Santorium, correspondence relating to personal possessions, memorial program, correspondence relating to grave markers and deed for plots in the Dale Cemetery Association, photographs of gravestones and a map of Warecrest, the estate of E. R. Ware in Yonkers, New York, additional correspondence. Also map of Woodlawn Cemetery, info on the Elisha Ware homestead, Edward James Ware info and cabinet card, article on Henry Ware, information on the Great Bed of Ware in England.Effinger and Smith family genealogy1985-199545
Scope and Contents
Information and family trees related to the Effinger family. Includes correspondence from and information on Robert C. Effinger and sons. Letters to his sister Katherine and to Barlow, some not related to genealogy. Last Will and Testament of Daniel Smith of Rockingham. The Frontiersman, the publication of the Society for Descendants of Colonel John Smith. Information and correspondence relating to the opening of the Woodrow Wilson Museum at his birthplace. The museum is in the Effinger family house. Items relating to Wildegg Castle.Wildegg Castle items199746
Scope and Contents
Postcards, photographs, publications and correspondence relating to the castle in Switzerland. The Effinger family owned the castle.Katherine Taylor Effinger items1921-195947
Scope and Contents
William Barlow Ware's wife and W. Barlow Ware Jr's mother. Includes birth and death certificates, trust indenture. Correspondence includes letters from W. B. Ware regarding their upcoming marriage, letters from doctors regarding Ware's arm. Letters from camp counsellors regarding Ware's progress, several from Camp Marienfeld in New Hampshire.Letters from W. Barlow Ware Jr. to his mother193448Letters from W. Barlow Ware Jr. to his mother193549Letters from W. Barlow Ware Jr. to his mother1936410Letters from W. Barlow Ware Jr. to his mother1937411Letters and writings of W. Barlow Ware Jr. as a childCirca 1935412European travel diary1956-1957413
Scope and Contents
Typescript copies, 191 pages, of a travel diary to Europe, namely France and Italy, England and Germany are also mentioned. It is unclear the connection to Ware, though Barlow Ware is mentioned as being on the trip within, or the exact names of those involved. A cover letter is from members of the Staley family of Wellesley Hills Massachusetts. A J P. Humphreys is the likely author, probably James Patton Humphreys, who had a sister named Mary Staley. The name Brud is also mentioned frequently in the diary as is his father, Pa. Brud's brother is listed as Pat, which is probably short for Patton. Pat's wife Jane is also mentioned, this is probably Jane Schroeder Humphreys. Brud was a teacher at the Belmont Hill School Massachusetts. Pat taught at South Kent School Connecticut. Both Brud and Pat studied at the Sorbonne and much of the diary is from France. The journey begins stateside on the way to New York and a booking on the ship Maasdam.Ruslan Samedov correspondence2002414John Hultgren correspondence2003415Miscellany416
Scope and Contents
Includes photographs probably of Camp Marienfeld in the 1930s. Ware with Touchdown the bear, birthday cards and other correspondence and photographs. Also A Prayer for Barlow Ware dated January 22, 2006.Ithaca Rotary Club NewsJuly 1990 - July 19914
Scope and Contents
Ware's personal bound copies of Rotary News, The Rotary Club of Ithaca, New York.Cornell piggy bankMU-24986Small male ballet dancer statueMU-24975Miscellaneous small items5
Scope and Contents
Includes Cornell seal gold cufflinks (MU-2496); various Cornell pins and fobs, many from Ware's undergraduate days, including wrestling club, manager fob, student council, Chi Phi (21351), Cayuga Society, Snoopy and Woodstock, Cornell bear, Boy Scouts of America James E. West Fellowship Award.Fiddle-On-Picton cabin painting19758
Scope and Contents
Painting by Joe Miller of Ware's cabin in Clayton New York on the St. Lawrence River. Ware would bring various Cornell affiliated individuals for retreats here.Charcoal portrait of Barlow Ware as a child1
Scope and Contents
Signed by the artist, possibly G. J. ShepherdSeries IV. Awards, Recognitions, CeremoniesAwards and Recognitions417
Scope and Contents
Includes items and articles relating to Chi Phi, 1992 Tompkins County Distinguished Citizen Award, Rotary Foundation Meritorious Service Award, Boy Scouts of America Silver Beaver Award.Blaschka Marine Invertebrate Collection project2006418
Scope and Contents
Binder of material discussing the restoration project and showing images of the unveiling celebration in Corson-Mudd Hall, November 2006.Blaschka Collections1993-2006419
Scope and Contents
Photographs, publications, clippings and other items relating to Blaschka glass collections at Cornell and Harvard and Ware and Ware family support of the them.Blaschka Glass Collection publications1940-1991420
Scope and Contents
Includes the book Glass Flowers in the Ware Collection by Fritz Kredel, postcards from the Corning Museum of Glass and the Cornell collections.Cornell Athletic Hall of Fame Induction2003421Cornell Foremost Benefactor Induction2005-2006422Frank H. T. Rhodes Exemplary Alumni Service Award Induction2007423Barlow Ware Bench Celebration2007424
Scope and Contents
Includes photographs by George Gull of the bench outside Teagle Hall placed in gratitude of Ware's love and service to Cornell.Memorial Service2012425
Scope and Contents
Planning documents, programs, speeches, remembrances, regarding Ware's memorial service. Also photographs from Thornrose Cemetery in Staunton Virginia showing the Effinger / Ware mausoleum and Ware's marker.
Video of the memorial service is available online here: A Celebration of the Life of Barlow WareOversize Cornell items8
Scope and Contents
80th birthday card signed by many people, photo of Ware on his stone bench signed by several people, Athletic Hall of Fame certificate,70th birthday card signed by many people1Silver trayMU-2481June 15, 1974426
Scope and Contents
Presented to Ware by the Chi Phi Charter House Association for devotion to the Xi chapter.Boy Scouts of America Silver Beaver AwardJune 18, 19804
Scope and Contents
Includes plaque and medal.Unsung Hero Award Rotary District 717 plaqueMU-24851985-1986427Boy Scouts of America Baden-Powell Council Distinguished Citizen AwardMU-249919927Horace E. Shackelton '19 Memorial Award for Track OfficialsMU-250019937Rotary Service Award plaqueMU-24871994427Ithaca Sunrise Rotary Honorary Member plaqueMU-2482June 29, 1994427Distinguished Service Award Rotary Foundation plaqueMU-24831999-2000427Lifetime Honorary District Governor Rotary International District 7170 plaqueMU-24842004427Rotary Foundation of Rotary International crystal dove paperweightsMU-2494-24955Rotary Foundation of Rotary International crystal paperweightsMU-24935Guardian Angel for the Ithaca Sun Rise Rotary Club plaqueMU-2486427Beaver medal on ribbonMU-2489428Luggage tagMU-2490428Ithaca centennial medalMU-2491428Athletic Hall of Fame badgeMU-2492428The Spirit and Voice of Cornell, granite plaque with image of WareMU-248820037 ======= Series I. Album containing programs and photographs relating to Ware's recognition event as a foremost benefactor of Cornell University. Includes photographs of Ware, former Presidents Frank H. T. Rhodes and Dale Corson, Andy Noel and many other friends and athletic and alumni affairs staff. Photos show the presentation of the Ezra Cornell statute and Ware's inscription on the Builder's Wall. Also a number of letters with memories and thanks to Ware. Some copies of photos from early in Ware's Cornell career as well.Series III. Includes letters relating to items in his home, his Rolodex cards, correspondence, some relating to the Cornell Wrestling team, invitation letter for his induction into the Quill and Dagger Society, photo of him as Touchdown the bear and a letter from Herb Roth Sr. about Ware being the bear.Barlow family genealogyUndated43

The Frontiersman, the publication of the Society for Descendants of Colonel John Smith. Information and correspondence relating to the opening of the Woodrow Wilson Museum at his birthplace. The museum is in the Effinger family house. Items relating to Wildegg Castle.Wildegg Castle items199746

Scope and Contents
Typescript copies, 191 pages, of a travel diary to Europe, namely France and Italy, England and Germany are also mentioned. It is unclear the connection to Ware, though Barlow Ware is mentioned as being on the trip within, or the exact names of those involved. A cover letter is from members of the Staley family of Wellesley Hills Massachusetts. A J P. Humphreys is the likely author, probably James Patton Humphreys, who had a sister named Mary Staley. The name Brud is also mentioned frequently in the diary as is his father, Pa. Brud's brother is listed as Pat, which is probably short for Patton. Pat's wife Jane is also mentioned, this is probably Jane Schroeder Humphreys. Brud was a teacher at the Belmont Hill School Massachusetts. Pat taught at South Kent School Connecticut. Both Brud and Pat studied at the Sorbonne and much of the diary is from France. The journey begins stateside on the way to New York and a booking on the ship Maasdam.Ruslan Samedov correspondence2002414John Hultgren correspondence2003415Miscellany416
